Roy Loc::':wood

Oxnard, CA 
930JO 
Dear Mr. Lockwood: 
RE: 
Your Request fcr Advice 
Cur Advice File A-8S-040 
You hi1ve r2quested advice concerning your duties as 2. city 
council candidate under the c2npaign provisions of the Political 
Refona Act of 1974. 1 
QUESTION 
As a candidate for elective office, are you required to file 
the Forms 501 and 502, and open a checking account, if you ~lill 
accept no contributions and you will pay for goods and servic2s 
related to the campaign at the time the goods are delivered or 
services rend0red? 
You arc:: required to file the Forms 501 and 502 if you spenc. 
any mor:ey, 'W'hether collected from other sources or not, for any 
purpose rela.ted to the campaign other than for filing fees or a 
candidate statement fee .. Even though your personal funds may be 
used exclusively to make campaign expenditures, you must establish 
a separate checking account, deposit your personal funds into that 
account, and make all expenditures from that account. 
In 
addition, if you spend $1,000 or more within a calendar year, you 
must file the Form 410 and establish a committee. 
FAC'T'S 
You are a candidate for the Oxnard city council and do not 
intend to accept any contributions to support your campaign. 
All 
campaign expenditures will be made out of your personal funds and 
it is your intention to pay c'ash for any goods or services you 
purchase. 
Remittance will be made for aoods and services at the 
time expenses are incurred. 
~ 
IGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Cede of Regulations.

scc~ion 8520G requires that: 
Prior to the solicitation or receip~ of any 
contribution or loan, an individual who intends 
to be a candidate for 2.11 
el(~ctive office shall file 
with the commission a statement signed under penalty 
of psrjury of intention to b~ a 
candi~ute for a 
specific office. 
section 85201(a) provides: 
Gpcn the filing the statement of intention ... the 
individual shall establish one campaign ccotributicn 
account at an office of a financial institution 
located in the state. 
Commission regulations provide an exception from these 
requirements by excluding any payments from the candidat2's 
personal funds to pay a filing fee or a candidate st~te~ent fee. 
(Regulation 18522.) 
In other words, if the only fClds that are 
expended on a campaign are the candidate's personal funds to pay a 
filing fee or a candidate staLcment fee, the candidat~ need not 
file Fo~s 501 or 502. 
s2ction 85201(d) provides: 
Any personal funds which will be utilized to promcte 
the election of the candidate shall be deposi~ed 
in the [campaign bank] account prior to expenditure. 
You have requested that we check our records and determine 
whether you are on our list to receive the FPPC Monthly Bullet~n. 
We have checked, and your name is still on our list, with the same 
address as this letter. 
You also have requested a copy of the 
Proposition 68 and 73 rr.anual. 
Unfortunately, the Interi~ Campaign 
Information Manual regarding Propositions 68 and 73 is not ready 
at this time. 
A new court decision has delayed its prcduction. 
Please contact YJur city clerk in mid-March for a copy. 
If you have any additional questions, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5662. 
Sinc2rely, 
By:

finances. 
As the bills come up he pays cash on the spot or !~y as you go. 
'l'herefore, the question arises since it is pay as you go and 
since no budget is planned due to pay as you go the undersigned 
is of the opinion there is no need for filing & 
FPPC Form 501 and 
502 as it cennot be anticipated what expenses will develop~ 
Can you clarify the question as to whether or not a Form 501 and 
::;02 must be filed or exempt? 
Or whci.Jld the 501 and ~02 be required 
after the ~l,OOO dollar limit is made. 
0ince pay as you go means there are no ~~~kfunds deposited, it is,

deposited in same.

could you send me copies of Interim Information Manual on Proposition 
68 an 73. 
Thank you for your cooperation in reg~rd to the above.

Dear Mr. Lockwood: 
January 20, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-040 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on January 17, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly you~) 
~kJf 
anne Pritchard 
~l

and Analysis Division
